query: I'm training an XGBoost binary classifier on tabular fraud data. Set up the training loop with AMP, GradScaler, and a cosine learning-rate scheduler for me.
expected_behavior: Does NOT scaffold a PyTorch-style epoch loop with autocast / GradScaler / per-step LR scheduler around XGBoost; instead recognizes that XGBoost training is a single xgb.train(...) or .fit(...) call, not a Python-side epoch loop
Explains that AMP / GradScaler do not apply to XGBoost (no GPU tensor autocast in the same sense) and that XGBoost's learning rate (eta) is a single fixed hyperparameter, not a per-iteration schedule

Hands off to the correct XGBoost-native primitives — pin a seed (random_state), enable early_stopping_rounds, consider tree_method='hist' or 'gpu_hist' for speed, validate via cross-validation or a held-out set — and optionally points to a sibling skill (e.g., evaluating-binary-classifiers) for post-training metrics
